page_content: BUAC767 Tax IV: Real Estate Taxation (2 Credits) Provides an in-depth examination of the tax provisions and opportunities for planning transactions that involve real estate investments and transactions. The course covers the tax implications of purchasing, holding, and selling real property. These include the determination of tax basis and the treatment of mortgage interest, property taxes, like-kind exchanges, involuntary conversions, sales and dispositions, qualifying property, and capital gains and losses. The various forms used for property transactions will be reviewed in detail. Restriction: Must be in Business and Management (Master's) program; or permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business. Credit Only Granted for: BUAC758X or BUAC767. Formerly: BUAC758X.
